problem: Implement CLI library commands for section-level feedback (Phase 2b). Prior runs implemented all files. The CLI references 'love' as a rating value — if LOVE rating display changes (heart to double thumbs up), SKILL.md may need updated descriptions.

files.why: Module router dispatching to list, show, and comments subcommands. Fully implemented.
Lists library items with ID, title, status, date in table format. Fully implemented.
Shows report with section headings annotated with [slug] and comment summaries. Fully implemented.
Nested router for comments subcommands (list, post). Fully implemented.
Lists comments grouped by section with author, rating, timestamp. Fully implemented.
Posts section rating. RATING_MAP on lines 5-11 includes 'love' -> 'LOVE'. Supports --reply-to for threading.
Multi-format item resolution: cuid ID, ticket short ID, title substring. Fully implemented.
Main dispatcher. Lines 94-98: 'library' case registered and routes to runLibrary.
Agent discoverability documentation. Library section (lines 146-172) documents all commands.

facts: All 7 new files exist and are structurally complete
Both modified files (src/index.ts, SKILL.md) have expected changes
Item resolution: 3 strategies — cuid (starts with 'c', 25 chars), ticket short ID (/^[A-Z]+-\d+$/), title substring
Auto-slugification: section flag with spaces gets slugified
hxFetch uses basePath '/api' for library endpoints
TypeScript strict mode, ES2022 target, Node16 module resolution
SKILL.md includes rating values: thumbs-up, thumbs-down, love
unknowns: Whether 'love' CLI alias needs any description update in SKILL.md given display changing from heart to double-thumbs-up
Whether rating should be optional for --reply-to posts in comments-post.ts (currently required via requireFlag)
